data.fundName: Bar Down Investments / Founders Development Company

reasons.title: Massive Job Catalyst
reasons.description: A new 1.7 million square foot Amazon Operations Center is currently under construction immediately adjacent to the property, serving as a massive employment catalyst.

reasons.title: High-Growth DFW Submarket
reasons.description: Cleburne is part of the DFW metroplex, the #3 fastest-growing metro in the US, with local household and employment demand far outstripping projected supply.

reasons.title: Institutional Debt Structure
reasons.description: The project is backed by a 40-year fixed-rate HUD 221(d)(4) loan at 86% LTC, effectively eliminating interest rate and refinancing risk.

summary.quote: 351-Unit Ground-Up, Class A Development Just South of DFW Metroplex with strong risk-adjusted returns and sponsor alignment.
paragraphs: RailRoad Lofts is a 351-unit ground-up, Class A multifamily development located in the growing city of Cleburne, Texas, just south of the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex. As part of the city's first mixed-use community, the property is positioned as a premier residential option walkable to restaurants, shopping, and entertainment.

The investment features a shorter 3-year target holding period compared to traditional multifamily developments, with projected average annual returns of 23%. The deal structure is bolstered by a fixed-rate HUD loan and the construction of a new 1.7 million square foot Amazon Distribution Center directly adjacent to the property.
summary.conclusion: This project offers high-end property amenities and energy-efficient construction, positioned toward strong leasing and long-term retention in a high-growth market.

cards.title: Financial Returns
cards.summary: The investment offers strong total returns and tax depreciation benefits with a shorter target holding period than traditional value-add multifamily projects.

cards.title: Property Overview
cards.summary: Cleburne's first master-planned community featuring 11 tenanted buildings, a resort-style pool, smart home tech, and energy-efficient construction.

cards.title: Market Analysis
cards.summary: Cleburne is a strategic DFW suburb with a technology-driven workforce and significant housing demand fueled by its proximity to Fort Worth and Dallas.

cards.title: Sponsor Profile
cards.summary: Led by seasoned entrepreneurs and developers with extensive backgrounds in construction management, tech-driven data analytics, and large-scale multifamily assets.

financialReturns.pageSubtitle: Detailed financial projections and investment structure

projections.label: Average Annual Return
projections.description: Projected average yearly return for Class A2 Limited Partners.

projections.label: Equity Multiple
projections.description: Projected total return on equity based on an August 2028 sale.

projections.label: Yield on Cost
projections.description: Stabilized net operating income divided by total project cost.

projections.label: Preferred Return (B2/C2)
projections.description: The annual priority return offered to Class B2 and C2 investors.

projections.label: Loan-to-Cost (LTC)
projections.description: The ratio of the HUD loan relative to the total replacement cost.

projections.label: Total Project Cost
projections.description: Total capitalization required for the 351-unit development.

uses.description: Includes cash to close loan and interest reserve
Includes pre-construction, developer, and management fees

sources.description: Includes both Limited Partner and Sponsor contributions

timeline.description: Investor portal opens, funds due by Dec 19, and HUD loan closing.
Groundbreaking and vertical construction. Depreciation benefits provided.
Lease-up begins in 2026, stabilization in 2027, and projected sale in August.

benefits.title: Cost Segregation
benefits.description: Accelerated depreciation through professional cost segregation studies.

benefits.title: Bonus Depreciation
benefits.description: Significant tax write-offs expected to be provided in tax years 2026 and 2027.

benefits.title: Early-Year Paper Losses
benefits.description: Tax benefits are provided early during the development and stabilization phases.

saleWaterfall.description: Return of all initial capital contributions.
Payment of any unpaid accrued preferred return.
Remaining proceeds split based on class-specific IRR hurdles (16% IRR).

cashFlowDistribution.description: Investors receive all cash flow until the Preferred Return is satisfied.
Profits split until a 16% Internal Rate of Return is achieved.
Profits split equally after investors surpass a 16% IRR.

data.summary: The Cleburne submarket shows strong stabilization with an average occupancy of 94% across recent deliveries. New Class A supply is achieving effective rents between $1.77 and $2.05 per square foot.

paragraphs: Bar Down Investments is a real estate investment firm led by Ashley Wilson and J Scott, specializing in the acquisition and operation of multifamily assets. The firm is partnered with Founders Development and Pomona Property Group to execute ground-up development projects in the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex.

The sponsor team brings a vertically integrated approach, combining institutional-level technology and data analytics with deep local development expertise. Their leadership includes a Graduate Master Builder and former President of the Greater Ft. Worth Builders Association, ensuring high-quality execution and strong community relationships.

description: Lead developer with 40 years of local Texas construction experience.

description: Hands-on construction oversight and design due diligence.

Experience spanning over 1,000+ units and $315M in transactions.

teamMembers.name: Ashley Wilson
teamMembers.title: Founder, Bar Down Investments
teamMembers.background: Ashley has been involved in over $165 million in transactions and manages over 1,000 multifamily units. She serves on the Penn State University Real Estate Board.

teamMembers.name: J Scott
teamMembers.title: Partner, Bar Down Investments
teamMembers.background: J spent 15 years in the tech industry managing large teams for Microsoft before completing $150M in real estate transactions and managing a 1,000+ unit portfolio.

teamMembers.name: Cary Clarke
teamMembers.title: Founder, Founders Development
teamMembers.background: Cary has over 40 years of experience in neighborhood development and luxury construction. He is a past President of the Greater Ft. Worth Builders Association.

partners.name: Pomona Property Group
partners.description: Provides analytical expertise and focuses on building long-term wealth through strategic property acquisitions.

partners.name: H2H Plans, LLC
partners.description: Designing spaces that bring vision to life, responsible for the architectural planning of RailRoad Lofts.

partners.name: BBG Real Estate Services
partners.description: Provides market survey data and real estate services to support underwriting and supply/demand analysis.

advantages.title: Decades of Execution Experience
advantages.description: The leadership team brings a combined 80+ years of experience in development and construction, specifically within the Texas market.

advantages.title: Superior Debt Structure
advantages.description: The project utilizes a HUD 221(d)(4) loan, providing a 40-year fixed rate that eliminates refinance and interest rate risk during the hold period.

advantages.title: Strategic Multi-Entity Partnership
advantages.description: The team combines Bar Down's acquisition and operational expertise with Founders Development's deep local construction roots in Fort Worth.

advantages.title: Institutional Asset Strategy
advantages.description: The sponsor's strategy focuses on ground-up Class A developments in high-growth DFW submarkets with immediate catalysts like the adjacent Amazon facility.
